alternatives: A Glue_n-exercising multi-observer variant (two observers plus nontrivial reconciliation) was considered and deferred to a possible follow-up packet; this packet keeps Glue_n present-but-unexercised as declared.
assumptions: A1 (definition): TI's RUN-0081 OnlineIssuance^LC seven-component signature and source gate, per the frozen blobs/TI-FORMAL-OBJECT.md.
A2 (construction_choice): the specific finite systems (alphabet {a,b}, Gamma_0={'a'}, MAXLEN=4, four stages, deterministic issue-all Iss_n); designed witnesses.
A3 (definition): the reconstruction model with declared hypothesis class H = {0,1,2,3}; REC-facts are relative to H.
A4 (implementation): the frozen fixture correctly implements A1 over A2/A3, carries w_n as explicit per-event trace fields, and enforces the [T]/[E]/[F] control discipline at exit.
A5 (construction_choice): Ext_n trivialized as declared in the instantiation map; Glue_n present but not exercised.

claim.independence_scope: The three evaluations share one implementation, one run, one baseline system, and one task vocabulary; they are not evidentially independent methods, and this packet asserts no independence result of any type.
claim.scope: One designed finite deterministic single-observer system family and one 30-token task vocabulary, at the pinned revision; exploration tier, formal-only grade; absorbed under the RUN-0081 gate clause 4; no physical substrate; no general theorem.
claim.statement: In the designed finite OnlineIssuance^LC instantiation, under the declared reconstruction model: (1) ALPHA (projection-only change Proj_{L=2} -> Proj_full) leaves the source issuance set exactly fixed while enlarging the observer's decidable set 25 -> 30, all five new decisions negative; (2) BETA (admissibility-only change Adm_1 -> Adm_2) enlarges the issuance set 9 -> 13 while the L=2 projected record and decidable set are bit-for-bit unchanged; (3) the same admissibility change under the fixed wider projection L=3 is observer-visible and simultaneously enlarges issuance and shrinks reconstruction decidability by one task (abbb).

alternatives: a genuinely witness-bearing extension category with morphisms and composition

construction_forks.selected: trivialized: finite (e, w) pairs over the gate-clause CandExt(Gamma_n); no categorical structure exercised (premise A5)

construction_forks.selected: H = {0,1,2,3} over the admissibility parameter k, declared before evaluation

premise_ledger.exact_statement: TI's RUN-0081 seven-component source object OnlineIssuance^LC = (Gamma_n, Adm_n, Ext_n, Iss_n, Proj_{o,n}, Glue_n, tau_n) with its source gate, including gate clause 2 witnesses w_n : Adm_n(e_n), as fixed in FORMAL-OBJECT.md.

The finite instantiation: binary alphabet {a,b}, Gamma_0={'a'}, MAXLEN=4, four stages, Adm_k(e) iff e novel and |e| <= 4 and #b(e) <= k, Iss_n issues all admissible candidates in sorted order deterministically; single observer; Glue_n present in the signature but not exercised.

The reconstruction model: the observer knows Gamma_0, CandExt, Iss, the stage count, and the projection rule, and knows Adm = Adm_k for some k in the declared hypothesis class H = {0,1,2,3} without knowing k; REC(s) is decided iff every k in H whose projected trace equals the observed one agrees on ISS(s). H is a declared free parameter; REC-facts are relative to H.

The frozen Python program blobs/ti_pit_02_fixture.py correctly implements the A1 signature instantiation over the A2 systems and the A3 reconstruction model, with per-event admissibility witnesses w = (novel, length, b_count) carried in tau_n, and with the [T]/[E]/[F] check discipline (1 [T], 4 [E], 6 [F]; every [F] failing-direction probe must fail for exit 0).

Ext_n is trivialized: represented by the finite set of pairs (e, w) over the gate-clause candidate object CandExt(Gamma_n), with no nontrivial categorical structure exercised; declared in the instantiation map, not silently dropped.

interfaces.requested_datum: any classification of the ALPHA/BETA/BETA-prime intervention triple under the receiver's preregistered gate machinery, subject to the authorship restriction in nonclaims

method_ledger.grade: computation (deterministic, stdlib-only Python 3; output frozen and hashed)
method_ledger.label: ALPHA evaluation (projection-side intervention: Proj_{L=2} -> Proj_full, issuance structure bit-identical)
method_ledger.result: The source issuance set is exactly fixed at 9 tokens (a [T] by-construction identity, protected by an [F] probe); the observer's decidable set grows 25 -> 30 of 30; the five newly decidable tasks (abb, aabb, abab, abba, abbb) are all decided negative.
method_ledger.verification: Re-run blobs/ti_pit_02_fixture.py; byte-compare stdout against blobs/ti_pit_02_output.txt (exit 0 requires all six [F] probes to fail).

method_ledger.grade: computation (deterministic, stdlib-only Python 3; output frozen and hashed)
method_ledger.label: BETA evaluation (admissibility-side intervention: Adm_1 -> Adm_2, projection bit-identical at Proj_{L=2})
method_ledger.result: The source issuance set grows 9 -> 13 tokens, each new issuance carrying its explicit trace witness; the observer's projected record and entire decidable set are bit-for-bit unchanged: through the fixed L=2 projection, BETA is invisible.
method_ledger.verification: Re-run blobs/ti_pit_02_fixture.py; byte-compare stdout against blobs/ti_pit_02_output.txt (exit 0 requires all six [F] probes to fail).

method_ledger.grade: computation (deterministic, stdlib-only Python 3; output frozen and hashed)
method_ledger.label: BETA-prime evaluation (same admissibility intervention under the fixed wider projection Proj_{L=3})
method_ledger.result: The same admissibility change is observer-visible (abb enters the record at stage 2) and shrinks reconstruction decidability by one task (abbb becomes undecidable: consistent hypotheses {2,3} disagree) while enlarging issuance 9 -> 13.
method_ledger.verification: Re-run blobs/ti_pit_02_fixture.py; byte-compare stdout against blobs/ti_pit_02_output.txt (exit 0 requires all six [F] probes to fail).

nonclaims: No physical or source-issuance significance: the fixture is absorbed under the RUN-0081 gate clause 4 (finite/computable/fixed-law) and must not be cited as evidence for Issue[S] of any type; Issue[S]^physical remains false.
No TI claim status changes: TI-C019 and TI-C020 are untouched; issuing this packet moves no TI verdict.

TI assigns no diagnostic label of any kind to ALPHA, BETA, or BETA-prime under any receiver taxonomy; the intervention descriptions are mechanical statements of which signature component was edited, and any label the receiver assigns is receiver-owned and must not be attributed to TI.
The [T]-tagged ALPHA issuance-invariance identity holds by construction and carries no evidential weight; it is excluded from the evidential headline (4 [E] + 6 [F]).
Three methods are not three independent evidential units (see evidence_structure).

This packet cites, scores, and advocates no normalization frame, receiver frame, or rival frame. Authorship restriction (carried from the governing referee, surviving issuance): this packet and any frame authored by its originating P2C lane may not be used together as a frame-discriminating pair in any receiver adjudication; the receiver's independent-reviewer and blind-issuer roles must be satisfied by other parties.
The designed witnesses do not establish that real systems exhibit these dissociations.
quantifiers: All computed facts are existential: they hold for the specific finite systems, projections, and declared hypothesis class H at the pinned revision; no universal claim over systems, thresholds, hypothesis classes, or task semantics is made.

The BETA invisibility fact (projected record and decidable set unchanged under Proj_{L=2}) is a parameter fact of L=2, not a structural necessity; the BETA-prime leg exhibits the same intervention as observer-visible at L=3.
Consistency and decidability sweeps are exhaustive over the declared finite hypothesis class H = {0,1,2,3} and the 30-token vocabulary only.

question.exact_question: In one finite deterministic instantiation of TI's OnlineIssuance^LC signature with a declared reconstruction hypothesis class H, what are the exact issuance-set and observer-decidability consequences of (ALPHA) changing only the observer projection Proj_{o,n} with issuance structure bit-identical, versus (BETA) changing only the admissibility predicate Adm_n with the projection bit-identical, including the same admissibility change under a fixed wider projection (BETA-prime)?

residuals: R-1: Glue_n is not exercised; any receiver analysis needing reconciliation structure requires a further packet.
R-2: REC-facts are relative to the declared hypothesis class H; sensitivity to other hypothesis classes is not swept.

R-3: The family is deterministic and single-observer, hence globally patchable by construction; case families with incompatible interface contexts are out of this fixture's scope (semantics body section 2.7).
R-4: The composite ALPHA-then-BETA system is not computed; interventions are evaluated separately from the shared baseline.

source.evidence_grade: formal only (finite executable toy fixture; absorbed under the RUN-0081 gate clause 4)

verification.replication_status: Replicated. Re-executed by the TI steward at adjudication and again from the frozen blob at issuance (2026-07-16): exit 0, stdout byte-identical to blobs/ti_pit_02_output.txt; script and output content hashes match the author's pinned values; the inlined script in the semantics body is byte-identical to the frozen blob; all six [F] failing-direction probes fail as required; the fixture lints clean under the receiver's tests/tef_check_tag_linter.py --strict (1 [T], 4 [E], 6 [F], 0 untagged).
